# docs(benefits): rewrite AgentOSOnYourCodebase.md (2/10 → concrete, honest, goose-bumps)

Part of #14310 · follow the content rubric pinned on #14310. Refs #14310.

**Problem:** `AgentOSOnYourCodebase.md` is ~2/10 — vague capability prose, no concrete proof, missing the local-model/self-heal value, and it leaves the Neural-Link bridge ambiguous (reads as if required).

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Real **problem-scope → how Neo resolves it → why you should care → what's in it for you**; concrete proof (mined from closed PRs/tickets); local-model + self-heal value.
- [ ] **Honesty boundary:** learning arbitrary codebases (Agent OS) does **NOT** require Neural Link; Neural Link is the masterpiece bridge for *Neo* UIs (conversational UIs, no browser reload) — super-cool, not a hard requirement. State it clearly.
- [ ] Goose-bumps bar; overcome "solves too much" skepticism with proof, not claims; proven-today vs trajectory kept honest.

**Files:** `learn/benefits/AgentOSOnYourCodebase.md`.

This guide rewrite modifies a public/human-consumed Benefits surface, so intake needs an explicit contract before implementation.

| Neural Link boundary in the guide | #14316; `learn/agentos/NeuralLink.md`; KB grounding | State that arbitrary-codebase learning does not require Neural Link; Neural Link is the live Neo UI possession bridge. | If the guide mentions live runtime possession, scope it to Neo UI/App Worker surfaces and future extension separately. | This guide file links to Neural Link | KB query + source read evidence |
| Local/remote model value in the guide | #14316; `learn/agentos/ModelProviders.md`; `learn/benefits/DeployingTheAgentOS.md` | Explain local providers as portability/privacy/cost/residency value, with remote providers as valid fast-start options. | Avoid implying Gemini-only or local-only deployment. | This guide file links to model/deployment docs | Source read evidence |
